nameMeanings.name: Foster
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Forster   ‘worker in a forest’.
English: perhaps a nickname from Middle English foster ‘foster parent’ (Old English fōstre, a derivative of fōstrian ‘to nourish or rear’). But other explanations are equally or more likely.
English: from Old French forcetier ‘maker of scissors’; see Forster 2.

variants.name: Fewster
meanings: English (mainly East Yorkshire and Durham): occupational name from Anglo-Norman French and Middle English fu(y)ster ‘maker of saddletrees’. See also Foster .

variants.name: Forster
meanings: English: shortened form of   Forrester  , an occupational name for someone who worked in a forest.
English: possibly a Norman French nickname or occupational name from Old French forcetier ‘maker of scissors or shears, cutler’. Compare Force and Forse .

German (mainly Förster): agent derivative of Middle High German forst ‘forest’, denoting someone who lived in or near a royal forest, or who worked in one (see Forst ). This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare Foerster .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: Robert Foster 13th ggf was born in 1482, in Birch, Essex, England, United Kingdom. He married Margaret Tendring in 1505, in Essex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 18 December 1545, in his hometown, at the age of 63.

ko.lifeSummary: Robert Foster 13th ggf(은)는 1482년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 비르치에서 태어났다. 그는 1505년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스에서 Margaret Tendring(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1545년 12월 18일 그의 고향에서 63세의 나이로 사망했다.
